front: What is the primary purpose of cache memory?
back: Cache memory acts as a high-speed buffer between the fast CPU and slower main memory, reducing memory access latency and improving system performance.

front: Define cache hit and cache miss.
back: A cache hit occurs when the CPU finds the requested data in the cache. A cache miss occurs when the data is not found, requiring a fetch from main memory.

front: What are the three main cache mapping techniques?
back: Direct Mapping (each block maps to one location), Fully Associative Mapping (any block can go anywhere), and Set-Associative Mapping (combines both approaches).
Temporal locality means that if a memory location is accessed, it is likely to be accessed again soon (recently accessed items tend to be accessed again).
Spatial locality means that if a memory location is accessed, nearby memory locations are likely to be accessed soon (accessing one memory location predicts access to adjacent locations).
Write-through writes data to both cache and main memory simultaneously, ensuring data consistency but slower writes due to main memory access.
Write-back writes data only to cache initially, marking the block as dirty. Main memory is updated only when the block is replaced, improving write performance.
A cache line is the minimum unit of data transferred between main memory and cache, typically 16-64 bytes.
LRU (Least Recently Used) replaces the block that has not been accessed for the longest time when a cache miss occurs and the set is full.

front: What are the typical cache levels in a hierarchy?
back: L1 (Level 1) is fastest and smallest, L2 is larger and slower, L3 (if present) is largest and slowest - forming a memory hierarchy.

front: What is the function of a cache tag?
back: A tag stores the portion of the main memory address that identifies which block is stored in each cache line, used for address matching during cache access.
Cache coherency ensures that multiple caches have consistent views of the same memory location, critical in multi-processor systems.

front: What is the advantage of set-associative mapping over direct mapping?
back: Set-associative mapping reduces conflict misses by allowing multiple blocks to map to the same set, while being less complex than fully associative mapping.
A victim cache is a small fully-associative buffer that holds blocks evicted from the L1 cache, reducing misses due to conflict.
